hypothesis: Introducing an energy-guided AMDEN by integrating a differentiable surrogate potential energy surface into the reverse diffusion objective to bias the denoising trajectory toward low-energy configurations will enable generation of relaxed, low-energy amorphous materials across multi-element systems without Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement, and this will be falsifiable if generated samples under energy-guided diffusion fail to exhibit lower potential energy or poorer agreement with MD-derived structural metrics compared with HMC-denoised baselines.
